title: Foundation and Makeup on Polyester
timeEstimate: 20–45 minutes; oil + pigment combo, polyester locks oil under heat
stainChemistry: Foundation, concealer, blush, BB cream, and similar liquid makeup are pigment (iron oxides, titanium dioxide) plus oil (silicone, mineral oil) plus emulsifiers and sometimes wax or polymer film-formers. The oil component penetrates the fiber while pigment particles lodge in the fiber's surface texture. Water-based foundations are easier to remove than oil-based; long-wear / 24-hour / matte foundations are harder because of polymer film-formers. Polyester is OLEOPHILIC (grabs the oil component aggressively) and heat-sensitive (hot water sets the oil into the fiber, hot dryer locks pigment plus yellows the fabric over time). Setting powders and primers add additional polymer layers that resist water-based cleaning. The recurring trap is rubbing — pushes pigment particles deeper into the polyester surface and makes the stain look set when it's not. Scrape gently first, then work from the back.
whyThisWorks: Powder preload (cornstarch / baking soda) absorbs the oil component before any moisture spreads the stain. Generous Dawn pretreat handles the oil + silicone matrix that polyester grabs onto. Cool oxygen-bleach soak finishes residual pigment. Cool wash plus AIR-DRY locks nothing. The honest cap: silicone-heavy primers and long-wear / matte foundations sometimes plateau on dyed polyester.
defaultAssumption: Foundation transfer onto a collar / shirt edge / sleeve / pillowcase. Standard liquid foundation = high-confidence DIY. Long-wear / 24-hour / matte formula = harder; pro for valuable garments. Hot-dried = often permanent yellow shadow.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Plastic spoon edge or dull butter knife
spottingProtocol.instruction: If makeup is thick on the surface, scrape excess off with a plastic spoon edge. Flat-edge work only. NEVER rub — rubbing pushes pigment particles into polyester surface texture and makes the stain look set when it's not yet.

spottingProtocol.technique: Pull bulk oil up before water
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cover the spot with cornstarch or baking soda. Press gently. 10–15 min absorb. Soft-brush off. Most surface oil comes off this way without water — water-first spreads makeup wider on polyester.
Generous Dawn directly on the spot. Soft toothbrush 10 min. Polyester is oleophilic — extended dwell needed for the oil + silicone matrix in foundation. Cool damp-cloth rinse.

spottingProtocol.agent: Test Oily Feel — Repeat Dawn If Yes
spottingProtocol.instruction: Touch the spot with a clean fingertip. Oily feel = repeat Dawn. Polyester typically needs 2 Dawn passes for makeup oil component.

spottingProtocol.agent: OxiClean Cool Soak Plus Cool Wash
spottingProtocol.equipment: OxiClean per label, COOL water, washing machine
spottingProtocol.instruction: OxiClean cool soak 30 min. Cool machine wash with normal detergent. SKIP fabric softener — coats fiber, blocks future cleaning. AIR-DRY. NEVER hot water — sets oil + locks pigment + yellows polyester.
AIR-DRY only. Inspect in daylight. Faint shadow = repeat Dawn pass. NEVER hot dryer until cleared — locks pigment and yellows polyester over time.

steps: 1. Scrape excess gently with plastic-edge. NEVER rub.
2. Cornstarch / baking soda preload 10–15 min, soft-brush off.
3. Generous Dawn pretreat, cool, 10 min, soft toothbrush.
4. Repeat Dawn pass if oily feel remains.
5. OxiClean cool soak 30 min + cool machine wash. No softener.
6. AIR-DRY + daylight inspect. Never heat until clear.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat sets oil and locks pigment on polyester
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets oil, locks pigment, yellows polyester
NEVER fabric softener — coats fiber, blocks cleaning
Always scrape gently, never rub — rubbing pushes pigment into fiber
Polyester is oleophilic — extended Dawn dwell + 2 passes needed
Long-wear / 24-hour / matte foundations — often plateau, pro for valuable garments
Powder preload BEFORE water — water-first spreads stain

homeSolutions.instruction: Foundation on polyester is fixable IF you do the powder step first. Cornstarch or baking soda pulls the oil component up before any water spreads the stain wider. Two hard rules: NO hot water (locks the oil and yellows polyester) and NO hot dryer until cleared. The other big trap is rubbing — pushes pigment into the polyester surface texture and makes the stain look permanent when it's not yet.

If there's a thick smear of foundation on the surface, scrape it off with a plastic spoon edge or dull butter knife. Flat-edge work only. NEVER rub. Rubbing is the #1 cause of foundation stains that look unrescuable but were just rubbed in.

homeSolutions.agent: Cornstarch or Baking Soda — 10 to 15 Minutes
homeSolutions.instruction: Cover the spot with cornstarch or baking soda. Press gently. 10 to 15 minutes. Soft-brush off. Most surface oil comes off this way without water.

Polyester is oleophilic — it grabs the oil and silicone in foundation tight. Apply a generous amount of Dawn dish soap directly on the spot. Work in with a soft toothbrush for 10 minutes. Cool damp-cloth rinse.

homeSolutions.agent: Test for Oily Feel — Repeat If Needed
homeSolutions.instruction: Touch the spot with a clean fingertip. Still feels oily? Repeat the Dawn step. Polyester typically needs 2 Dawn passes for foundation oil. The second pass usually does the job — silicone-heavy primers are the harder case and may need a third.

homeSolutions.agent: OxiClean Cool Soak and Cool Wash
homeSolutions.instruction: Mix OxiClean per label in COOL water. Soak the garment 30 minutes. Then cool machine wash with normal detergent. SKIP fabric softener — it coats the fiber and blocks any future cleaning. NEVER hot water — sets oil, locks pigment, yellows polyester.

homeSolutions.agent: AIR-DRY and Inspect Before Any Heat
homeSolutions.instruction: Hang to AIR-DRY. Inspect in daylight before the dryer. Faint shadow remaining? Repeat the Dawn pass. NEVER hot dryer until cleared — heat locks residual pigment and yellows polyester over time.

steps: 1. NO hot water. NO hot dryer until cleared.
2. Scrape excess gently with plastic-edge. NEVER rub.
3. Cornstarch / baking soda preload 10–15 min, soft-brush off.
4. Generous Dawn pretreat, cool, 10 min, soft toothbrush.
5. Repeat Dawn pass if oily feel remains.
6. OxiClean cool soak 30 min + cool wash, no softener.
7. AIR-DRY + daylight inspect. No dryer until cleared.
products: Plastic spoon edge / dull butter knife
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot water or hot dryer was already used (often permanent yellow shadow). Stop on long-wear / 24-hour / matte foundation on dyed polyester — plateau likely, pro for valuable garments. Stop on specialty / performance polyester (pro). Stop after 3 home cycles without progress.
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets oil + yellows polyester
NEVER fabric softener — coats fiber, blocks cleaning
Always scrape gently, NEVER rub — rubbing pushes pigment into fiber
Polyester is oleophilic — 2 Dawn passes typical

neverDo: Never rub — pushes pigment into polyester surface texture
Never hot water on makeup + polyester
fiberSensitivities: Polyester is oleophilic — grabs foundation oil + silicone tightly
Polyester yellows under heat over time
Polyester typically needs 2 Dawn passes for makeup oil
Long-wear / 24-hour / matte foundations: often plateau because of polymer film-formers
Performance / specialty polyester: pro — coatings react unpredictably

materialWarnings: Scrape gently, never rub — rubbing pushes pigment into fiber
Powder preload before water — water-first spreads it wider
No hot water / no hot dryer — polyester locks oil + yellows
Long-wear formulas often plateau on dyed polyester

escalation.when: Hot water or hot dryer already used (often permanent yellow shadow). Long-wear / 24-hour / matte foundation on dyed polyester (likely plateau). Performance / specialty / dry-clean-only polyester. Rubbing has pushed pigment into fiber surface. 3 home cycles plateau. Silicone-heavy primer residue.
escalation.whatToTell: "Foundation / makeup on [white / dyed / athletic / specialty] polyester, formula [standard liquid / long-wear / matte / mineral / silicone-primer], age [fresh / hours / hot-dried suspected], home-treated with [scrape + cornstarch preload + Dawn cool × 2 + OxiClean cool soak + cool wash + air-dry]. No hot, no rubbing. [Residual shadow / yellow shadow / hot-dried suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with solvent spotting capability for combo stains

contextQuestions: Standard liquid foundation, long-wear / 24-hour / matte, mineral, or silicone-heavy primer?
Fresh, hours old, or hot-dried already?
Has anyone rubbed the stain trying to clean it?
Color of foundation — light, medium, deep?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Long-wear / matte / 24-hour foundations are the hard subset because of polymer film-formers — often plateau on dyed polyester. Standard liquid foundations respond well to the powder + Dawn × 2 + OxiClean sequence. Silicone-heavy primers are an underrated cause of plateau — silicone resists water-based cleaning. Rubbing during home attempts pushes pigment into polyester's surface texture and is the #1 cause of foundation stains that look permanent but aren't.
